title: Bulk Edit Line of Business and Topics for Metrics
story: As a DataTools Pro user managing a large Metrics Glossary, I want to bulk-select multiple metrics and assign Line of Business or Topic categories in a single action, so that I can efficiently categorize my metrics without editing each one individually.
description:
Managing categories across a large number of metrics is time-consuming when limited to single-record editing. This feature extends the existing multi-row selection pattern (already available on object fields) to the Metrics Glossary, allowing users to select multiple metric rows and bulk-assign Line of Business or Topic categories.

## Behavior

1. User selects multiple metric rows using **Ctrl+click** (individual) or **Shift+click** (range).
2. An **"Edit X Metrics"** button appears in the toolbar, displaying the count of selected records. The button includes an HTML id attribute for analytics tracking.
3. Clicking the button opens a popup where the user selects a category type (Line of Business or Topic).
4. The user checks the desired category values and clicks **Assign** to apply them to all selected metrics.
5. After assignment, selected rows remain selected and a success toast is displayed so the user can continue editing.
6. The popup stays open until the user explicitly closes it (close icon or clicking outside), allowing sequential edits across category types.

## Constraints

- **Bulk selection is disabled when Group By sorting is active**, since grouped views can duplicate metrics across categories and create ambiguity.
- **Line of Business and Topic must be edited independently** to prevent inadvertent clearing of unrelated category assignments. Editing topics does not affect existing Line of Business assignments, and vice versa.

## Data Model

No schema changes. Uses existing category assignment tables. Bulk assignment deletes previous assignments for the selected category type on the selected rows and replaces them with the new checked values.
acceptance_criteria: User can multi-select metric rows using Ctrl+click (individual) and Shift+click (range selection)
An 'Edit X Metrics' button appears in the toolbar showing the count of selected records
Edit button has an HTML id attribute for analytics tracking
Clicking the edit button opens a popup with category selection (Line of Business or Topic)
User can check multiple category values and click Assign to apply to all selected metrics
Line of Business and Topic are edited independently — editing one does not clear the other
Bulk selection is disabled when Group By sorting is active
Selected rows remain selected after assignment completes
A success toast is displayed after bulk assignment
Popup remains open after assignment for continued editing across category types
Closing the popup via close icon or clicking outside dismisses it
ux_changes: Adds multi-select checkboxes to the Metrics Glossary table with a floating bulk-action toolbar. An 'Edit X Metrics' button shows the count of selected records. Clicking opens a category assignment popup with separate sections for Line of Business and Topic. After assignment, a success toast appears and rows stay selected. The popup remains open for sequential category edits.
middleware_changes: New batch-update endpoint accepts an array of metric IDs with target category type and values. Server-side logic deletes existing category assignments for the selected type on selected rows, then inserts the new checked values. Exception handling covers the bulk operation.
api_changes: AJAX endpoint for bulk category assignment — accepts selected metric IDs, category type (LOB or Topic), and checked category values. Returns updated metric data for table refresh.
implementation_notes: Implemented via multi-row DataTables selection with a dynamically shown edit button. AJAX call to server prepares a modal with all available taxonomy values. On assign, all previous category assignments for the selected category type on selected rows are deleted and replaced with the new selections. Exception handling implemented for the bulk operation. The edit button includes an HTML id attribute for analytics tracking. Bulk selection is explicitly disabled when Group By sorting is applied to avoid ambiguity from duplicated metrics across categories.
comment_summary: Developer implemented multi-row select with a category assignment modal. Product owner refined the UX to use a prominent button showing record count instead of a small icon, with an analytics-tracking id. Testing revealed that editing both categories at once could inadvertently clear data, leading to a design decision to separate Line of Business and Topic editing into independent operations. A follow-up enhancement kept rows selected after assignment and kept the popup open for sequential edits, displaying a success toast instead of auto-closing.
